sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines (LUV) Stock: Soars as Company Announces Major Austin Expansion and 2,000 New Jobs
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines (LUV) is significantly expanding its operations in Austin, opening a new crew base in March 2026 that will eventually create 2,000 jobs. This expansion includes a new training facility and will increase Austin's nonstop destinations to 53, bolstering the airline's long-term strategy and regional economic impact. Southwest’s stock closed higher at $41.17 following the announcement, reflecting market confidence in the growth plans.

feed.title: Deadline Soon: Baxter International, Inc. (BAX) Investors Who Lost Money Urged to Contact The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz About Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz is reminding investors of Baxter International, Inc. (BAX) about an upcoming deadline to participate in a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Baxter made misleading statements regarding defects in its Novum IQ Large Volume Pump, which led to patient injuries and deaths. Investors who purchased BAX securities between February 23, 2022, and October 29, 2025, are urged to contact the firm before the December 15, 2025, lead plaintiff deadline.

feed.title: ANTITRUST—9th Cir.: Apple’s epic battle with Epic Games continues with Ninth Circuit mostly affirming contempt order
feed.summary: The ongoing legal battle between Apple and Epic Games continues as the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals has largely affirmed a contempt order against Apple. This decision indicates that the complex antitrust case remains active, with the court upholding most of the prior ruling against the tech giant.

feed.title: The Five Areas Every Shop Must ‘Rock’: Sherwin-Williams’ Todd Ahrens Explains the Relationship Advantage
feed.summary: Todd Ahrens, with 35 years in the collision repair industry, emphasizes that strong relationships are the foundation for a successful shop, not equipment or DRP status. He identifies five critical areas—labor, estimating, parts, scheduling, and workflow—where cultivating relationships directly leads to improved efficiency, quality, and profitability. Ahrens argues that these relational dynamics are crucial for modern collision repair, influencing everything from tech performance to customer trust and vendor support.

feed.title: Bank of America (BAC) Stock After the Bell Dec. 12, 2025: Record Close, Analyst Target Changes, and What to Watch Before the Next Market Open
feed.summary: Bank of America (BAC) stock closed at a record high of $55.14 on December 12, 2025, up 1.06%, marking a significant recovery since the 2008 financial crisis. This rise was influenced by positive analyst target changes, particularly from RBC, and a favorable macro environment for the banking sector. Investors are now focused on upcoming macro data and interest rate discussions for future stock movements.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs (GS) Stock After Hours: What Happened After the Bell on Dec. 12, 2025—and What to Know Before the Next Market Open
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs (GS) experienced a slight increase in after-hours trading on December 12, 2025, following a significant pullback during the regular session amid broader market jitters over AI valuations and a recent Federal Reserve rate cut. The article details GS's performance, relevant analyst target changes, and the impact of the Fed's decision on bank stocks. Investors should monitor upcoming economic data and the "AI trade" spillover effect, as global market sentiment and bond yields will dictate GS's performance heading into the next market open on Monday, December 15, 2025.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase Stock After Hours (Dec. 12, 2025): JPM Holds Near 52-Week Highs as Wall Street Slips — What to Know Before the Next Market Open
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase (NYSE: JPM) shares held near 52-week highs on December 12, 2025, closing up 0.36% to $318.52, despite broader market declines driven by AI valuation concerns and inflation worries. The bank's stock was virtually unchanged in after-hours trading, and investors are weighing potential 2026 expense increases against strong investment banking and markets performance. Analysts generally maintain a positive outlook, though price targets are being adjusted in light of expense debates and interest rate dynamics.

feed.title: Caterpillar, NVIDIA Corp. share losses lead Dow's 287-point fall
feed.summary: Caterpillar Inc. and NVIDIA Corp. experienced significant stock losses, contributing to the Dow Jones Industrial Average's 287-point decline. These individual stock movements highlight a negative trend within the broader market index.

feed.title: Future use of McDonald's site in Ocean City remains unclear
feed.summary: The longtime McDonald's restaurant at Ninth Street and West Avenue in Ocean City is closing, with conflicting reports on the exact closure date. The future of the prime property, located in a busy commercial district, is uncertain, as neither the owner nor McDonald's corporate has announced plans for redevelopment or sale. The mayor has offered support for affected employees, including a potential job fair.

feed.title: UBS Raises Price Target for Halliburton (HAL) to $32 While Maintaining Neutral Rating | HAL Stock News
feed.summary: UBS has increased its price target for Halliburton (HAL) to $32.00 from $24.00, while reiterating a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst a series of updated price targets from other analysts, reflecting a generally positive outlook on the company's prospects. Halliburton, a leading oilfield-services company, is recognized for its strong position in hydraulic fracturing and completions.

feed.title: Lithium Royalty Corp. Congratulates Power Metals on Offtake Agreement with Albemarle; Power Metals Reaffirms 2026 Production Target
feed.summary: Lithium Royalty Corp. (LRC) congratulates Power Metals Corp. on securing a cesium concentrate offtake agreement with Albemarle Corporation, a major critical minerals producer. This agreement includes a prepayment of up to C$5 million, supporting Power Metals' goal of 2026 production from its Case Lake project. LRC holds a 2.0% gross overriding royalty on the Case Lake project, which contains a significant inferred cesium mineral resource, positioning LRC to benefit from near-term cesium production.

feed.title: Bridgewater Bancshares president Chybowski sells $94k in stock
feed.summary: Joseph M. Chybowski, President & CFO of Bridgewater Bancshares Inc (NASDAQ:BWB), sold 5,211 shares of common stock for $94,053 and exercised options for 6,000 shares for $44,820 on December 10, 2025. This comes as the company recently reported Q3 2025 earnings that missed analyst expectations, leading to a stock price drop despite some analysts maintaining positive ratings.

feed.title: Super Micro (SMCI) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
feed.summary: Shares of Super Micro (SMCI) fell 4% after underwhelming earnings from Oracle and Broadcom shifted investor focus from "growth at any cost" to "prove the returns" in AI-linked stocks. Oracle's revenue miss and increased capital expenditures, coupled with Broadcom's warning of gross margin pressure, sparked a macro rotation away from AI infrastructure. Despite the dip, Super Micro's stock is up 7.7% year-to-date, though still significantly below its 52-week high.

feed.title: Cramer's week ahead: New economic data and earnings from FedEx, Jabil
feed.summary: Jim Cramer outlined significant economic data releases and key earnings reports for the upcoming week, emphasizing the importance of data collection amidst shifting market dynamics. He highlighted nonfarm payroll and retail sales reports, as well as earnings from major companies like FedEx, Jabil, and General Mills, providing his insights on their potential market impact. Cramer also touched upon the recent decline in tech stocks and the relevance of different sectors in the current economic environment.

feed.title: Amgen’s Uplizna Approved For Its Largest And Most Competitive Indication Yet
feed.summary: Amgen's drug Uplizna has received approval for generalized myasthenia gravis, marking its third indication and entry into a highly competitive market. Despite substantial competition, Amgen is confident in Uplizna's potential, positioning it as a new standard of care and expecting rising sales. This approval expands Amgen's rare disease portfolio.

feed.title: CCC Intelligent Solutions Sees Positive Momentum Amid Market Dynamics
feed.summary: CCC Intelligent Solutions Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: CCC) recently saw its stock trade up by 6.37% due to positive sentiment following corporate advancements and strategic enhancements. While the company exhibits weaknesses in pretax profit margins, its strong gross margin of 73.8% and healthy liquidity suggest potential for growth. Investors are watching for a breakout above $7.8 to confirm a bullish trend, with overall sentiment remaining cautiously optimistic despite financial performance lagging sector benchmarks.

feed.title: U.S. Equity ETF Tracker | Tech stocks drag Nasdaq lower, triple inverse Nasdaq ETF rises nearly 6%; Cannabis stocks rally across the board, MSOS ETF surges 54%; Nuclear energy shares plummet, double inverse OKLO ETF jumps over 30%.
feed.summary: U.S. stock indexes closed lower, with tech stocks weighing heavily on the Nasdaq. Conversely, cannabis stocks experienced a significant rally, driven by news of potential relaxation of marijuana restrictions. Nuclear energy stocks plummeted, causing inverse ETFs tied to the sector to surge.

feed.title: Explore Sectors Beyond Tech With Active Value ETFs
feed.summary: This article suggests that investors should look beyond large-cap tech growth strategies and consider active value ETFs, especially in sectors like financials and energy. The BNY Investments team highlights that value companies historically perform better during inflation and points to the BNY Mellon Dynamic Value ETF (BKDV) as an example of a fund using a bottom-up approach to select undervalued companies with sound business fundamentals. BKDV currently has a significant allocation to the financial sector and also includes energy stocks, positioning it well for current market conditions.

feed.title: Cronos Group Expands Across Europe: Market Buzz
feed.summary: Cronos Group's stock price has risen by 11.93% due to significant investor optimism following its strategic expansion into Europe. The company plans to acquire CanAdelaar, Europe's largest adult-use cannabis sector player, for $67 million, and has also unveiled new Lord Jones Live Resin Fusions pre-rolls in Canada. This move aims to boost Cronos's European footprint and capitalize on evolving cannabis markets.

feed.title: C3is Inc. completes $9 million registered offering of units and warrants
feed.summary: C3is Inc. (NASDAQ:CISS) has finalized a registered offering of 7,500,000 units, raising approximately $9 million before fees and expenses. The offering included both common units and pre-funded units, each comprising common shares and various warrants with specific exercise terms. This financial move was detailed in a recent SEC filing by the Athens, Greece-based deep sea freight transportation company.

feed.title: COGT Boom: Is It Time to Cash In?
feed.summary: Cogent Biosciences (COGT) stock is up following promising clinical trial results for bezuclastinib, leading analysts like H.C. Wainwright to raise price targets. The company has also strengthened its financial position with recent public offerings, positioning it for future growth with upcoming FDA submissions and commercial launches. Despite significant R&D costs, Cogent Biosciences demonstrates strong liquidity and strategic financial management.

feed.title: Linde Stock (LIN) After Hours on 12.12.2025: Why Shares Jumped 3% and What to Watch Before the Next Market Open
feed.summary: Linde plc (LIN) shares rose 3.21% on December 12, 2025, closing at $416.24, an outperformance driven by a cluster of fresh analyst notes and investor event takeaways that reframed the narrative around growth and valuation. Analysts reiterated "Outperform" ratings and high price targets for Linde, with a focus on its EPS growth algorithm, backlog visibility, and new "Growth6" strategy. Investors are advised to watch after-hours activity, analyst target clustering, macro risks, and the corporate calendar for the next trading session.

feed.title: DEFT ALERT: Kirby McInerney LLP Reminds DeFi Technologies Investors of Important Deadline in Class Action Lawsuit
feed.summary: Kirby McInerney LLP is reminding investors of DeFi Technologies (NASDAQ:DEFT) who suffered losses to contact the firm regarding a securities fraud class action lawsuit. Investors have until January 30, 2026, to apply to be lead plaintiff. The lawsuit alleges that DeFi Technologies made misleading statements about its DeFi arbitrage strategy, understated competition, and was unlikely to meet its revenue guidance for fiscal year 2025, leading to significant stock price declines after negative announcements in November 2025.

feed.title: ProPetro enters power supply agreement with Coterra's unit
feed.summary: ProPetro Holding Corp's energy unit, PROPWR, has signed an agreement with a unit of Coterra Energy to supply power for microgrids in New Mexico's Permian Basin, a project set to begin in Q1 2026. This deal increases ProPetro's 2026 capital spending estimate from $200-$250 million to $250-$275 million due to additional equipment orders, signaling a growing trend for U.S. firms to adopt on-site power generation to reduce fuel costs and enhance reliability.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley's Katerina Simonetti talks her 2026 market outlook
feed.summary: Katerina Simonetti, Executive Vice President at Morgan Stanley Private Wealth, discusses her market outlook for 2026. She provides insights on how investors can construct their portfolios for the upcoming year, offering strategic advice on navigating potential market changes.

feed.title: LTTE: GE Fired me for defending my family — Why that should scare every worker.
feed.summary: A GE Lynn welder, Dan Reynolds, shares his story of being fired by General Electric after defending his transgender sister in a private Facebook group. He argues that his dismissal for an off-the-clock act, unrelated to his work, sets a dangerous precedent for all workers, allowing corporations to police personal opinions and stifle free expression. Reynolds emphasizes that this issue transcends political divides, impacting fairness, freedom, and dignity for every employee.

feed.title: Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) Director Sells $13,910.40 in Stock
feed.summary: Moderna Director Abbas Hussain sold 504 shares of MRNA stock on December 9th for $13,910.40, reducing his position by nearly 25%. This comes as Moderna reported a Q4 EPS of ($0.51) and revenue of $1.02 billion, beating estimates, despite a 45.4% year-over-year revenue decline and negative net margins. Analysts currently have a "Reduce" rating on Moderna with a consensus price target of $29.46.

feed.title: CompoSecure Faces Legal Challenges Over Proxy Statement
feed.summary: CompoSecure (CMPO) is facing legal challenges from shareholders who allege that its Proxy Statement, related to a $3.953 billion deal with Husky Technologies, contains incomplete and misleading information. To avoid further litigation, CompoSecure has chosen to voluntarily supplement the Proxy Statement while denying any wrongdoing. Despite these legal hurdles, the acquisition is seen to provide a positive outlook for future growth, though some financial and valuation concerns remain.

feed.title: Ascendis Pharma Poised for Growth After Price Target Upgrade and Strong Trial Results
feed.summary: Ascendis Pharma (ASND) is showing strong potential for growth, with its stock trading up by 4.66% after positive FDA designations and favorable trial results for TransCon CNP. Analysts have upgraded price targets, forecasting significant peak sales for Yorvipath, and the company reported a substantial increase in Q3 revenue. Despite some financial challenges typical of biotech, the market shows bullish momentum and optimism for future regulatory approvals and commercial success.

feed.title: Is Ascendis Pharma’s Surge Sustainable?
feed.summary: Ascendis Pharma A/S (NASDAQ: ASND) saw its stock surge by 4.88% due to strong Q3 2025 results and positive pipeline developments for TransCon CNP, despite an FDA PDUFA extension. Analysts from Wolfe Research and Stifel have issued "Outperform" and "Buy" ratings with price targets around $255-$256, underscoring the company’s potential for significant sales growth. However, a notable debt of EUR 365M and negative capital stock and equity indicate financial hurdles that require careful navigation.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Lowers Price Target for General Mills (GIS) to $50
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has maintained its 'Equal-Weight' rating for General Mills (GIS) but lowered its price target from $53 to $50, a 5.66% decrease. This adjustment follows several other analysts lowering their price targets for GIS in recent months, indicating a cautious outlook for the company. Despite the downward adjustments, Wall Street analysts project an average target price of $53.86 for GIS, implying an upside of 15.35% from the current price.

feed.title: Jack Corrigan Buys 5,000 Shares of American Homes 4 Rent (NYSE:AMH) Stock
feed.summary: Director Jack Corrigan recently purchased 5,000 shares of American Homes 4 Rent (NYSE:AMH) for $114,800, increasing his direct holdings by over 40%. The company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.30 and reported strong Q3 earnings, beating analyst estimates. Despite an average "Moderate Buy" rating and a price target of $38.14 from analysts, the stock has experienced recent price target cuts from some firms.

feed.title: Halliburton Fourth Quarter 2025 Earnings Conference Call
feed.summary: Halliburton Company (NYSE: HAL) will host a conference call on January 21, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. CT to discuss its fourth quarter 2025 financial results. A press release detailing the earnings will be issued prior to the call and made available on Halliburton's website. The call will also be accessible via live webcast, with a recorded version available for seven days afterward.

feed.title: Cooper Companies appoints Brian G. Andrews as principal accounting officer
feed.summary: Cooper Companies announced that Brian G. Andrews, the company's Executive Vice President, Chief Financial Officer, and Treasurer, has taken on the additional role of principal accounting officer. This change, effective Tuesday, was detailed in a recent SEC filing. Andrews assumes these responsibilities from Agostino Ricupati, who will continue with the company as Senior Vice President, Tax.

feed.title: Caterpillar (CAT) Stock After the Bell on Dec. 12, 2025: Why Shares Dropped, After-Hours Move, Analyst Forecasts, and What to Watch Before the Next Market Open
feed.summary: Caterpillar (CAT) stock closed down 4.43% on December 12, 2025, largely due to a broader market risk-off sentiment rather than specific company news. While the company faces ongoing macro uncertainty and tariff pressures, analyst forecasts show a mixed outlook with near-term earnings dips but strong growth projections for 2026. Investors should monitor weekend macro headlines, interest rate expectations, and commodity prices before markets reopen on Monday, December 15, 2025, with the next major catalyst being estimated Q4 earnings on January 29, 2026.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials chief strategy officer Stanley G. Bass to retire in April 2026
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials Company (NYSE:VMC) announced that Stanley G. Bass, its chief strategy officer, will retire on April 30, 2026. This announcement, made via an SEC filing on December 10, indicates that his salary proration and incentive opportunities will remain unchanged until his retirement. The company maintains a strong market position and has reported positive financial results, with its stock trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker VMC.

feed.title: Skyworks Solutions approves executive incentive plan for fiscal 2026
feed.summary: Skyworks Solutions, Inc. (NASDAQ:SWKS) has announced the approval of its Fiscal Year 2026 Executive Incentive Plan, designed to reward senior management, including named executive officers, based on corporate performance metrics. The plan ties cash incentives to the achievement of annual corporate goals, primarily focusing on revenue and non-GAAP operating income targets. Awards can reach up to 160% of base salary for the CEO and up to 100% for the CFO, with maximum payouts of double the target for exceeding performance.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands CEO Goldstein sells $26.6 million in stock
feed.summary: Robert G. Goldstein, CEO of Las Vegas Sands (NASDAQ: LVS), sold approximately $26.6 million worth of company stock across December 10 and 11, 2025, at prices between $66.39 and $67.03 per share. Concurrently, Goldstein exercised options to acquire 399,900 shares for $20.13 million. Despite the sale, the company shows strong financial health with a "GREAT" score, a P/E of 29.16, and robust gross profit margins of 79.81%, while analysts have raised price targets based on strong performance in Singapore and Macau.

feed.title: Bausch + Lomb allocates $2.8 billion to refinance outstanding loans By Investing.com
feed.summary: Bausch + Lomb Corporation announced it is allocating $2.8 billion in new term B loans to refinance existing debt obligations, aiming to replace outstanding term B loans due in 2031 and 2028. This strategic move is expected to reduce interest margins and maintain the maturity date for some loans while extending others, with the transaction anticipated to close in the first quarter of 2026. The parent company, Bausch Health Companies, maintains a strong financial health score and appears undervalued according to InvestingPro.

feed.title: UBS Raises Price Target for EQT to $76 While Maintaining 'Buy' Rating | EQT Stock News
feed.summary: UBS analyst Josh Silverstein has increased the price target for EQT (EQT) to $76 from $67, while reiterating a "Buy" rating for the stock. This adjustment reflects a 13.43% rise in the price target and continued confidence in EQT's market performance. The revised target is higher than the average Wall Street analyst target of $64.39, but the GuruFocus GF Value suggests a potential downside from the current price.

feed.title: 2026 Compliance Guide
feed.summary: Brown & Brown's Regulatory and Legislative Strategy Group has developed the 2026 Employee Benefits Compliance Guide. This guide assists with legislative and regulatory requirements for employee health and welfare benefit plans, covering topics like ACA, ERISA, COBRA, HIPAA, and Medicare requirements.

feed.title: BNY Joins US Govt Investment Initiative, Expanding Access To Savings For Workers
feed.summary: BNY (NYSE: BK) has announced its participation in a U.S. government investment initiative for children, specifically matching the federal government’s $1,000 contribution for eligible newborns of its U.S. employees. This program, part of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act passed by Congress and signed by President Trump, provides a $1,000 pilot contribution into a tax-advantaged account for eligible children born between 2025 and 2028. BNY's involvement aims to double the initial investment, building on its efforts to enhance employees' financial opportunities.

feed.title: Adamas Trust Declares Quarterly Cash Dividend
feed.summary: Adamas Trust (ADAM) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.23 per share for its common stock, effective for the quarter ending December 31, 2025. Additionally, dividends were announced for several series of its preferred stock. This action reinforces the company's commitment to shareholder returns, making it attractive to income-focused investors, despite a "Neutral" rating from Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, which noted mixed financial performance and high leverage concerns.

feed.title: Join Class Action to Recover Losses from DeFi Technologies (DEFT) - Contact Levi & Korsinsky Before January 30, 2026
feed.summary: Levi & Korsinsky LLP has filed a class action securities lawsuit against DeFi Technologies (NASDAQ:DEFT) to recover losses for shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between May 12, 2025, and November 14, 2025. The lawsuit claims that the company made false statements and/or concealed issues regarding its DeFi arbitrage strategy, competition, and its ability to meet revenue guidance for fiscal year 2025. Shareholders who suffered losses during this period are encouraged to contact Levi & Korsinsky LLP before January 30, 2026, to learn about their rights to seek recovery.

feed.title: Aterian receives Nasdaq notice for minimum bid price non-compliance
feed.summary: Aterian, Inc. (NASDAQ:ATER) has received a notice from Nasdaq for non-compliance with the minimum bid price requirement, as its stock closed below $1.00 for 30 consecutive business days. The company has a 180-day period until June 8, 2026, to regain compliance by having its shares trade at or above $1.00 for at least 10 consecutive business days. This news follows a recent report of significant revenue drop in Q3 2025 and the initiation of a strategic review process.

feed.title: Alignment healthcare COO Burzacchi sells $242k in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Alignment Healthcare's COO, Sebastian Burzacchi, sold $242,387 worth of common stock to cover tax obligations from vested restricted stock units, not as a discretionary trade. This news comes as Alignment Healthcare surpassed Q3 2025 earnings expectations, leading to upgraded price targets and ratings from Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan due to strong performance and projected Medicare Advantage membership growth. Analysts have a positive outlook on the company's future prospects.

feed.title: Apollo Global Management approves equity awards and deferral options for executives
feed.summary: Apollo Global Management (NYSE:APO) announced the approval of restricted share units (RSUs) for two senior executives, Martin Kelly and Whitney Chatterjee, valued at $10 million and $13.5 million respectively, with vesting tied to performance and long-term holding requirements. Additionally, the Compensation Committee approved options for executives to defer equity award settlements, with Scott Kleinman having already deferred a significant portion of his 2021 RSU awards until 2029 and 2032. These actions aim to align executive compensation with long-term value creation and shareholder interests, building on Apollo's strong financial health and consistent dividend payments.

feed.title: Mink Brook Asset Management buys DLH Holdings (DLHC) shares for $5082
feed.summary: Mink Brook Asset Management LLC, a ten percent owner in DLH Holdings Corp (NASDAQ:DLHC), has acquired 924 shares for $5,082 on December 11, 2025, increasing its total stake. The transaction comes after DLH Holdings reported an unexpected decline in fourth-quarter earnings and revenue for fiscal year 2025.

feed.title: Aramark refinances $2.38 billion in term loans with new 2030 maturity
feed.summary: Aramark Services, Inc. has refinanced $2.38 billion in outstanding U.S. Term B-8 Loans with new U.S. Term B-10 Loans, maturing in June 2030. The new loans bear interest based on SOFR or a base rate option with initial margins of 1.75% and 0.75% respectively, and require quarterly principal repayments starting in December 2028. This move follows Aramark's Q4 2025 earnings which missed expectations, leading to varied analyst responses but continued confidence in its long-term growth.

feed.title: CVB Financial Corp. Announces 145th Consecutive Quarterly Cash Dividend
feed.summary: CVB Financial Corp. has announced its 145th consecutive quarterly cash dividend of $0.20 per share for the fourth quarter of 2025. Thedividend was approved by the Board of Directors on December 12, 2025, and will be payable on January 13, 2026, to shareholders of record as of December 29, 2025. This reflects the company's consistent strong financial performance.
